exemplar.title: Float & Flourish - Building skills for life in water!

detailedExemplarData.title: Float & Flourish - Building skills for life in water!

strengths: Effective evaluation of source credibility through the OPCVL table
Creative problem-solving shown in the pivot to poster design and WhatsApp promotion strategies
Robust integration of primary interviews and secondary research to inform project decisions
Strong communication narrative with concrete examples of stakeholder collaboration
weaknesses: Insufficient detail on how insights from video-call meetings informed adjustments to the project plan
Session plans lack explicit assessment indicators to determine when participants have mastered each skill
Reflective discussion of social skills would benefit from examples of conflict resolution to illustrate adaptability
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The application of ATL skills is well evidenced through credible source evaluation, creative marketing pivots, and rich research integration. To deepen this section, **link meeting insights directly to project changes**, **embed mastery criteria within session plans**, and **include conflict or negotiation examples** to showcase interpersonal adaptability.

strengths: Clear connection of the learning goal to personal experience and community need
Well-sequenced session schedule that logically progresses from planning through execution
weaknesses: Lacks measurable, quantifiable success criteria (e.g., target participant numbers or skill benchmarks)
Omission of detailed success-criteria descriptors for the highest performance band (7â8)
Action plan missing specific dates, sequencing details, and resource allocations
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: **Planning demonstrates strong alignment with personal and community contexts and a coherent session sequence.** However, the framework would be considerably strengthened by **defining measurable success criteria** and **completing all performance bands** in the success-criteria table. Adding **specific timelines and resource assignments** will make the plan more actionable and trackable.

strengths: Demonstrated community engagement growth supported by participation metrics
Detailed time-reflection aligning performance bands with concrete examples
Clear evidence of reaching the target audience of eight participants
Compelling photographic documentation of participantsâ skill progression
weaknesses: Lacks a thorough evaluation of the product against each defined success criterion
Reflective framework omits explicit consideration of personal learning and IB learner-profile traits
Limited discussion of personal development areas such as safety management and responsibility
Success-evaluation table is incomplete and omits holistic criteria like the impact on self and learning
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The reflection effectively highlights community impact, timeline alignment, and skill development through strong evidence. To elevate this section, **evaluate the product against every success criterion**, **integrate reflections on personal growth and IB learner-profile attributes**, and **complete the success-evaluation table** to ensure a holistic analysis.

annotations.comment: Your learning goal is clearly tied to personal experience and community need, but it lacks measurable success criteria (e.g., number of sessions, participant skill levels). Add quantifiable targets to better track progress.
The success-criteria table currently defines only the 0 band. Complete criteria descriptions for bands 1â8 to provide a clear roadmap for achievement and self-evaluation.
Your extended success-criteria table omits the 7â8 band. Define highest-level descriptors to guide your end-goal performance and ensure alignment with IB expectations.
The OPCVL table effectively evaluates source credibility. Enhance this by cross-referencing alternative perspectives from non-governmental or academic sources for balance.
Your use of creative thinking to pivot to poster design and WhatsApp promotion is a strong example of problem-solving. Linking turnout data directly to this strategy would illustrate its impact.
The video-call screenshot shows engagement, but the narrative should specify how insights from this meeting altered your project plan or tools.
Excellent integration of primary interviews and secondary research in your reflection. To deepen this further, critically assess how interviewee biases could affect your coaching approach.
The action plan uses monthly labels but would benefit from specific dates, sequence order, and resource allocations to make the plan more feasible and easier to monitor.
Your session schedule is well sequenced from planning to execution. Consider adding expected learning outcomes for each session to strengthen its alignment with your success criteria.
Session plans are clear but lack explicit assessment indicators. Include criteria for when a participant has âmasteredâ each skill to guide your instructional decisions.
Your reflection narrates growth but would be more robust if you evaluated your product against each success criterion with specific evidence for every band.
The success-evaluation table covers time, audience, and function. Add a criterion for âImpact on self and learningâ to complete your holistic reflection.
Your communication-skills narrative demonstrates clear stakeholder collaboration. Cite specific committee feedback that directly shaped your workshop design to strengthen this evidence.
Your social-skills reflection is strong; consider reflecting on a conflict or differing expectation you resolved to illustrate adaptability and negotiation depth.
You effectively show community engagement growth. Gathering direct testimonials from participants or parents would strengthen evidence of lasting impact.
Including the gala photo is a powerful collaboration example. Enhance this by explaining how you coordinated logistics or managed timelines during the event.
Your time-reflection aligns performance bands with evidence of participation and coach preparation. Link back explicitly to your action plan timeline for coherence.
You clearly demonstrate reaching the target audience of eight participants. Reflect further on how trial participants influenced program design beyond the core group.
Your photographic evidence of technique progression is compelling. Consider quantifying skill improvements (e.g., average lap time reduction) to deepen your evaluation.
The impact reflection describes client survival and performance. Enhance this by reflecting on your own development in safety management and responsibility.
Your conclusion is comprehensive. To link it more closely to reflection, tie specific examples to IB learner-profile traits (e.g., how you demonstrated âreflectiveâ or âopen-mindedâ).
